title: EDPB Personal Data Breach Notification (template v1.0)
description: Machine-readable JSON Schema derived 1:1 from the EDPB template v1.0 (adopted 2026-06-08; public consultation until 2026-08-05). Cell-level extraction from the official .docx, double-parsed and reconciled. Each field annotated with its GDPR article anchor (x-gdpr-ref). Source: https://www.edpb.europa.eu/our-work-tools/our-documents/other/template-personal-data-breach-notification_en
x-note: 126 numbered rows = 100 input fields + 26 section/structural rows.

field_3.title: Type of notification
field_3.description: Indicate here if you notifying a new data breach or if you are amending a previous notification

field_4.title: Sub-type of notification
field_4.description: Indicate here if you provide a complete notification, amend a preliminary (incomplete) notification or provide preliminary (incomplete) information to be amended.Complete (all required information can be provided)Incomplete (not all information can be provided right now)Withdraw (Cancel a previous Notification; e.g. duplicate, no risk after initial assessment)
field_4.x-visible-if: Withdraw visible only if "Type of notification" = "follow-up notification"

field_5.title: Reasons for withdrawing a previous notification
field_5.x-visible-if: visible only if "Type of notification" = "follow-up notification" and "Sub-type of follow-up notification" = "Withdraw"

field_6.title: ID of previously notified personal data breach
field_6.description: Please provide the ID of the previous personal data breach your are completing or amending.
field_6.x-visible-if: visible only if "Type of notification" = "complementary/amended notification/withdraw". Check existence of a previously notified data breach

field_7.title: Data controller's internal reference number
field_7.description: Provide your internal reference number if any

field_11.title: Identifier
field_11.x-visible-if: optional field, Ask for selected identifier

field_15.title: Type of organisation
field_15.description: If you consider your organisation not fitting in those four categories, please select “Others” and specify how.

field_18.title: Name of representative in EEA
field_18.x-visible-if: only if controller not established in EEA

field_19.title: Contact details* of representative in EEA

field_24.title: Description of the function of the reporting person

field_25.title: National identification number
field_25.x-visible-if: Optional field, implement only if required

field_26.title: Accuracy & Responsibility
field_26.x-visible-if: Optional field, implement only if required

field_29.title: Is a data protection officer designated?
field_29.x-visible-if: only if DPO not provided as reporting person in 1, yet

field_30.title: Name of the DPO
field_30.x-visible-if: only if DPO not provided, yet

field_31.title: Contact details*
field_31.x-visible-if: only if DPO not provided, yet

field_33.title: More information about the incident can be obtained at

field_38.title: Are other parties such as data processors or joint controllers involved?
field_38.description: Indicate here if other organisations were involved in the data breach (for example a data processor, a joint controller, etc.)

field_39.title: Qualification of the other involved party
field_39.x-visible-if: visible only if "Are other parties such as Data Processors or Joint Controllers involved?" = yes

field_40.title: Name of the other involved party
field_40.x-visible-if:
visible only if "Are other parties such as Data Processors or Joint Controllers involved?" = yes
Allow multiple

x-options.b: From a date to a date
x-options.c: From a date and is still ongoing

field_44.title: When did the personal data breach occur?
field_44.description: If you do not know the dates precisely, please indicate the estimated dates below
field_44.x-visible-if: “ f) To be determined” only available for incomplete notification.

field_45.title: Only estimated dates are possible
field_45.description: Please indicated how you estimated the dates of the breach in field 49

field_46.title: Beginning date and time of personal data breach

field_47.title: Ending date and time of personal data breach

field_48.title: Date and time of awareness of the data controller of the personal data breach

field_49.title: Reasons for late notification of the personal data breach
field_49.x-visible-if: Visible only if "date of awareness of breach" + 72h > current time and "new notification"

x-options.b: Detection and communication by the data processor

field_50.title: How was the personal data breach discovered?

field_51.title: Further description of the discovery of the personal data breach
field_51.description: Please describe how the personal data breach was discovered, e.g., Malware or Intrusions detecion system, Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure, …

field_52.title: Date of notification by other party
field_52.description: If you do not know this date precisely, please indicate the approximate date.
field_52.x-visible-if: only if external notification (50 is b), c), d), e), f))

field_53.title: Further comments on timeline and additional information
field_53.description:
Please provide an overview over relevant events and other issues regarding the timeline (if applicable), such as, the duration of impacts on availability, integrity and confidentiality, the awareness date of the processor, etc.
Please indicated how you estimated the dates of the breach (if applicable)

x-options.a: Confidentiality breach - where there is an unauthorised or accidental disclosure of, or access to, personal data.
x-options.b: Integrity breach - where there is an unauthorised or accidental alteration of personal data.
x-options.c: Availability breach - where there is an accidental or unauthorised loss of access to, or destruction of, personal data.

field_55.title: Nature of the personal data breach

x-options.a: Personal data are exfiltrated or disclosed
x-options.b: Personal data are likely exfiltrated or disclosed (no evidence)
x-options.c: Personal data are NOT exfiltrated NOR disclosed (reasonable evidence present)

field_56.title: Type of confidentiality breach
field_56.x-visible-if:
only if confidentiality
”To be determined” only available for incomplete notification.

x-options.a: Yes and protection is still intact (e.g. data was securely encrypted or otherwise protected)
x-options.b: Protective measures had been taken, but it is likely that they could be  subverted  (e.g. because the unauthorised party could be able to use decryption keys or exploit a technical weakness in the measures that have been applied)

field_57.title: Was the data unintelligible to anyone who was not authorised to access it  or were individuals not identifiable?
field_57.description: If you answered d), please specify why the assessment is not possible (e.g. technical reasons)
field_57.x-visible-if:
only if confidentiality
”To be determined” only available for incomplete notification.

x-options.a: Alteration or modification of the data, with no evidence of illegal or wrong use
x-options.b: Alteration or modification of the data, with evidence of illegal or wrong use, but with the possibility of reversing/recovering the damages to data subjects
x-options.c: Alteration or modification of the data with evidence of illegal or wrong use, without the possibility of reversing/recovering damages to the data subjects

field_59.title: Type of availability breach
field_59.description: Please assess the availability issue form the data subject’s perspective

x-options.a: Abuse of access privileges by employee
x-options.e: Security vulnerability (likely) exploited (known CVE or zero day)
x-options.f: Unauthorised access to personal data in IT systems
x-options.g: Data exfiltration / unauthorised extraction of personal data
x-options.h: Data of wrong data subject shown (mix-up of records)
x-options.j: E-waste (personal data still present on obsolete device)
x-options.k: Incorrect disposal of personal data on paper
x-options.n: Incorrect access permissions (e.g., cloud storage, shared folder)
x-options.o: Paper lost or stolen or left in insecure location
x-options.q: Personal data displayed to wrong recipient
x-options.s: Sending email to multiple recipients without blind copy / with open distribution list
x-options.v: Unintended publication (e.g., public link / public website / shared drive)
x-options.w: Verbal unauthorised disclosure of personal data

field_61.title: Cause of the personal data breach
field_61.x-visible-if: ”To be determined” only available for incomplete notification.

field_62.title: Further description of the nature of the incident
field_62.description: Please describe the nature of the breach (detection, awareness, cause, measures in place) shortly and provide on what facts your decision is grounded. If possible indicate the root cause of the incident.

field_63.title: Description of the systems, software, services, and infrastructures involved in the personal data breach and where they are located
field_63.description:
Please list the descriptions them in semantic groups, e.g. 

Systems:
• production database :
  o client database (full/partial) that countains ****, located in ***
  o internal HR database (full/partial) that countains ****, located in ***
• test infrastucture
  o testing database (full/partial) that countains ****; located in ***
Software:
• Accounting software
• Payment system software

x-options.i: Vulnerable individuals (elderly, refugees, disabled people, victims, …)
x-options.j: Additional concerned data subjects (please specify in further description)

field_65.title: Categories of concerned data subjects
field_65.description: If you answered i), please indicate what kind of vulnerable people. If you are not sure if or how the concerned data subject are vulnerable, please describe them shortly and why you would consider them as vulnerable.
field_65.x-visible-if: ”Not yet known” only available for incomplete notification.

field_66.title: Further description of categories of concerned data subjects and additional data subjects

field_67.title: Data subjects concerned by the personal data breach
field_67.x-visible-if: ”Number to be determined ” only available for incomplete notification.

field_68.title: Number of data subjects concerned by the personal data breach
field_68.x-visible-if:
Only if a/b selected

ask according to selected value before, Tooltip: “if c) the current estimate is: …”
field_68.x-required-if: if ‘exact number’ or ‘approximate number’ in previous question

x-options.a: Basic data (e.g. name, surname, date of birth)
x-options.b: Contact details (e.g. address, phone number, e-mail)
x-options.d: Criminal convictions, offence or security measures
x-options.f: Data revealing racial or ethnic origin
x-options.g: Data revealing religious or philosophical beliefs
x-options.h: Data revealing sex life or sexual orientation
x-options.k: Health data (e.g. medical records, test results, …)
x-options.m: Employment related health data (e.g. proof of sick leave, proof of medical attendance, results of medical fitness tests or similar)
x-options.n: Identification data (e.g. National identification number, ID number)
x-options.q: Payment methods (e.g. credit card, bank accounts)
x-options.r: Profile data (e.g. social network, credit score, psychology or other profile
x-options.s: s) User credentials (e.g. usernames, passwords, tokens)
x-options.u: Additional data types (please specify in further description)

field_70.title: Type of breached data
field_70.x-visible-if: ”Not yet known” only available for incomplete notification.

field_71.title: Further description of breached data and additional data categories

field_72.title: Personal data records concerned by the personal data breach
field_72.description: Please specify the type of record: number of data about each concerned data subject. For instance, to avoid just mentionning “500”. Because “5 types of data (namely *,*,*,*,*,*) about 100 people” and “100 types of data (namely *,*,*,*,*) about 5 people” may not lead to the same risks assessment, even if it could both be sumed up to “500 records”.
field_72.x-visible-if: ”Number to be determined ” only available for incomplete notification.

field_73.title: Number of personal data records concerned by the personal data breach
field_73.description: Please indicate here the number of records concerned by the breach, meaning the number of single entries in the database concerned by the breach.
field_73.x-visible-if: ask according to selected value before

x-options.d: Data protection and information security policies
x-options.l: Other measures (please specify in further description)

field_75.title: Relevant measures in place when the personal data breach occurred
field_75.description:
This list is not exhaustive and just covers common measures. 

You may have not applied some measures because they were not applicables or useful.

At the same time, you may have applied non-listed measures that were useful. If so, please specify them with answer L.

field_76.title: Further description of relevant measures in place when the personal data breach occurred
field_76.description: Reminder: You indicated that technical measures are likely to be subverted by the adversaries. Please add the relevant level of detail here to these measures, along the description of the other relevant measures.
field_76.x-visible-if: If 57 indicated that measures in place are likely to be subverted, remind DCs here to detail on this in the tooltip

x-options.a: Data were disclosed beyond the scope of the privacy policy or relevant legislation
x-options.b: Data may be linked, without unreasonable effort, with other information regarding data subjects
x-options.c: Data may be used for purposes other than the intended purpose or unlawfully

field_79.title: Likely consequences in case of breach of confidentiality
field_79.x-visible-if:
visible only if "Confidentiality"= yes
”Under assessment ” only available for incomplete notification.

x-options.a: Data may have been modified and used even though it is no longer valid
x-options.b: Data may have been modified into otherwise valid data and subsequently used for other purposes

field_81.title: Likely consequences in case of breach of integrity
field_81.x-visible-if:
visible only if "Integrity"= 1
”Under assessment ” only available for incomplete notification.

x-options.b: Service disruptions and difficulties in service use

field_83.title: Likely consequences in case of breach of availability
field_83.x-visible-if:
visible only if "Integrity"= 1
”Under assessment ” only available for incomplete notification.

x-options.a: Loss of control of their personal data
x-options.i: Loss of confidentiality of personal data protected by professional secrecy
x-options.j: Disclosure of data to unauthorised third parties
x-options.m: Material or non-material damage to property
x-options.n: Other impact (please specify in further description)

field_85.title: Nature of the potential impact for the data subject
field_85.description: Please indicate here the nature of the impact on the data subject rights and freedoms.

field_86.title: Further description of the other impacts for the data subjects
field_86.x-visible-if: visible only if "Description of the potential impact for the data subject" = "other"

field_87.title: Severity of the potential impacts
field_87.description: Indicate here the result of your self-assessment of the severity of the impact of the breach for the data subjects. Please select the highest severity if multiple potential impacts can occur.

x-options.a: The personal data breach is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons
x-options.b: The personal data breach is likely to result in a (not high) risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons
x-options.c: The personal data breach is unlikely to result in a risk to the rights and freedoms of natural personsc) Additional information is needed to assess the risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons

field_89.title: Outcome of the risk assessment carried out by the controller
field_89.x-visible-if: ”Additional information is needed to assess the risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons ” only available for incomplete notification.

field_90.title: Further description  of the risk assessment carried out by the data controller
field_90.description: Please describe the metodology used and the relevant factors you put into account.

field_92.title: Measures taken to address the personal data breach and to mitigate its consequences
field_92.description: Please indicate to what extend the measures resolve the issue.

x-options.e: Data protection and information security policies
x-options.o: Other measures (please specify in further description)

field_94.title: Relevant measures taken to prevent similar personal data breach

field_95.title: Further description of relevant updated permanent measures to avoid similar incidents in the future
field_95.description:
Please indicate to what extend the measures resolve the issue and improve the situation.

A description is expected if you answered with “Other measures” in the previous question m).

Nevertheless, do not hesitate to describe further measures taken after the breach, or to be taken, if you think it is relevant.

x-options.b: No, but it will be communicated on a specific date b1) No, but it will be communicated on a date to be determined
x-options.c: No, the investigation is still ongoing
x-options.d: No, it will not be communicated since the personal data breach is unlikely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons
x-options.e: No, it will not be communicated since one of the conditions referred to in article 34(3) of the GDPR is met

field_97.title: Has the personal data breach been communicated to data subjects?
field_97.description:
If you have not assessed yet if you are going to notify, please note that you will have to make a follow-up notification once you have make your decision

If you communicate the data breach to the data subjects in phases or if you cannot reach out to all data subjects, yet. Please indicated that in field 98 and 98a.
field_97.x-visible-if: No, the risk assessment is still ongoing’ only available for incomplete notifications.

field_98.title: Date of when information was given to data subjects
field_98.description: Please indicate here the date on which you have started to inform the data subjects.
field_98.x-visible-if: visible only if "information of data subject" = Yes

field_99.title: Future date on which the data breach will be communicated to the data subjects
field_99.x-visible-if: visible only if "information of data subject" = "No, but it will be communicated"
field_99.x-required-if: One of the fields is mandatory if "information of data subject" = "No, but it will be communicated"

x-options.a: The controller has implemented appropriate technical and organisational protection measures, and those measures were applied to the personal data affected by the personal data breach, in particular those that render the personal data unintelligible to any person who is not authorised to access it, such as encryption.
x-options.b: The controller has taken subsequent measures which ensure that the high risk to the rights and freedoms of data subjects referred to in paragraph 1 is no longer likely to materialise.
x-options.c: It would involve disproportionate effort. In such a case, there shall instead be a public communication or similar measure whereby the data subjects are informed in an equally effective manner.

field_100.title: Further information about conditions referred to in article 34(3) of the GDPR that are met
field_100.x-visible-if: visible only if "information of data subject" = "No they will not be informed"

field_101.title: Further description why the conditions referred to in article 34(3) of the GDPR that are met and the measures taken
field_101.x-visible-if: visible only if "information of data subject" = "No they will not be informed"

x-options.a: All data subjects have been informed
x-options.b: Not all data subjects have been informed

field_102.title: Number of data subjects informed
field_102.x-visible-if: visible only if "information of data subject" = Yes

field_103.title: Further description why not all data subjects have been informed
field_103.description:
Please specify the reason why some data subjects have not been informed, e.g. some might not have been because reason A, some other because reason B, etc.

Reasons can be of various natures and not just the technical ones have to be considered: ongoing process, some data subjects cannot be reached the same way, additionnal laws apply to them, the cost of information is much higher, etc
field_103.x-visible-if: visible only if "b) Not all data subjects have been informed”

field_104.title: Means of communication used to inform the data subject
field_104.x-visible-if: visible only if "information of data subject" = Yes

field_105.title: Further description of the other means of communication
field_105.x-visible-if: visible only if "information of data subject" = Yes

field_106.title: Content of the information provided to the data subjects
field_106.description: Please provide this content in the most relevant way. For example, if the provided information is just an email with only plaintext, copying the text here is sufficent. If, for instance, your information contains pictures/videos useful for understanding, please provide the means for the data protection authority to consult it (website page, email screenshots, download page, etc).
field_106.x-visible-if: visible only if "information of data subject" = Yes

field_109.title: Has the incident been reported to the police/judicial authorities as a criminal offence?

field_110.title: Has the incident been notified to other supervisory authorities or control bodies under other relevant legislation? (e.g. national cyber security centre, etc.)

field_111.title: Further information about notification to other authorities or bodies
field_111.description: Please specify which authority (about data protection or not) and if possible, provide the case ID that this authority used and then can be referred to.

field_113.title: Does the personal data breach involve cross-border processing carried out by a controller established in the European Economic Area (EEA)?
field_113.x-visible-if: SECTION: Only if DC is based in EEA

field_115.title: List of EEA countries where the controller has an establishment
field_115.description: Indicate here the countries concerned by the breach.
field_115.x-visible-if: visible only if "Is this notification a cross border notification made to your lead supervisory authority" = Yes

field_116.title: List of EEA countries where affected data subjects are located
field_116.description: Indicate here the countries concerned by the breach.
field_116.x-visible-if: visible only if "Is this notification a cross border notification made to your lead supervisory authority" = Yes

field_117.title: Approximate number of data subjects per country

x-options.a: Dated copy of the communication to the datasubject
x-options.c: Dated copy of the research report into the data breach (cyberincidents)
x-options.f: Dated copy of the internal procedure to notify a data breach
x-options.g: Dated copy of the internal policy to delete/destroy (outdated) personal data
x-options.h: Dated copy of the communication to the wrong recipients

field_125.title: Please identify the attachments to this notification
field_125.description:
Tooltip: with regard to phishing there needs to be a split-up of communications, namely, 3 categories: 
The owner, datasubject of the mailbox
Data subjects that received a phishing-mail

Data subjects of which personal data was in the compromised mailbox
